questions.prompt: You're starting a new OpenAI-powered project in 2026. Which API should you default to?

choices.text: Chat Completions — it's been around longer.
The Responses API — OpenAI's current primary interface. New features ship to it first, and it handles tool use, conversation state, and reasoning models more cleanly.
Assistants API — managed agents make everything easier.

questions.explanation: Default to Responses for new code. Chat Completions is still fine and not deprecated, but Responses is where OpenAI is investing — built-in tools, conversation persistence, agent loops, reasoning support are all cleaner. Assistants is legacy. Realtime is voice-specific.

questions.prompt: You have working Chat Completions code in production. Should you migrate to Responses?

choices.text: Yes, immediately — Chat Completions is deprecated.

Probably not just yet. Chat Completions is fully maintained and not deprecated. Migrate when you actually need a Responses-specific feature (multi-step tool use, reasoning models, conversation persistence, built-in tools). Don't migrate just to migrate.
Yes, but only the simple endpoints.

questions.explanation: Chat Completions isn't deprecated — it's still production-supported. The cost of migration is real (testing, behavioral diffs, regression risk). The benefit is zero unless you need a Responses-only feature. Migrate when you have a concrete reason, not on principle.
